authorization: Authorize S10-D2 continuation-track selection plan only.

candidate_origin.binding_constraint_evidence: OOS 3-year window produced 53 closed trades; K9 threshold = 100; trade density 88% of IS preserved

option_1_execution_authorization_prerequ.description: What a future Authorize Option 1 / OOS cost-stress sweep block would need to specify (informational only; this plan does NOT authorize execution).
option_1_execution_authorization_prerequ.expected_runtime_seconds: approximately 240 (4 tiers x ~80s per P10 reference)
expected_verdict_options: S0/S2/S3 all positive AND no new K12/DR fires --> OOS_COST_STRESS_SURVIVES
S2 or S3 flips negative OR DR2/DR3/DR5 fires --> OOS_COST_STRESS_BREAKS_AT_TIER_X
mixed --> case-by-case interpretation per pre-anchored enum
required_specifications: Cost tiers to run (recommend S0, S2, S3; S1 already covered by P10)
Whether to include S4 (currently reserved/out-of-scope per Tier-N spec)
Single-shot OOS invariant (no iteration based on results)
No threshold loosening on K12 / DR2 / DR3 / DR5
Output paths (recommended: reports/external_research_hunter/s10_d2_p10_5_oos_cost_stress_sweep_report.{json,md} OR another non-colliding path)
Pre-stage empty check + explicit git add discipline

option_1_oos_cost_stress_sweep.A_informational_gain: HIGH per unit cost. Produces orthogonal evidence to K9 sample-size: tests whether the directionally-positive OOS result degrades under cost stress the same way IS did. If S2/S3 stay positive on OOS, the OOS direction is more credible; if S2 or S3 flips negative on OOS, the OOS-window-length explanation gets weaker and cost sensitivity becomes a new candidate binding constraint.
option_1_oos_cost_stress_sweep.B_reuse_of_sealed_infrastructure: HIGHEST. Reuses: OOS driver (sealed at f0b3721), OOS cache (already validated by P10), S10-D2 Tier-N spec, runner_main.CONFIG, all S10-D2 plan-lock and phase-2 anchors. No new code surface.
option_1_oos_cost_stress_sweep.C_risk_profile: LOW. No fetch. No Databento. No API key access. No new strategy code. The only new artifact is the result report. Runtime ~4x P10 (which was 81.8s), so ~5-6 minutes total. Pre-stage discipline + parallel-session race risk would need monitoring, but no execution-side risk.
option_1_oos_cost_stress_sweep.D_directness_vs_binding_constraint: INDIRECT. Does NOT directly add OOS sample-size. Does NOT lower K9. Adds ORTHOGONAL evidence about cost-robustness on the OOS regime that informs interpretation of the existing 53-trade sample.
option_1_oos_cost_stress_sweep.E_time_to_result: Same day. Run + seal + commit within minutes.
option_1_oos_cost_stress_sweep.F_chain_of_custody_integrity: EXTENDS the S10-D2 chain. Same anchors, same Tier-N spec, same drivers. The new artifact slots in at PHASE2-S10-D2-XAD-RC-P10.5 or similar.
option_1_oos_cost_stress_sweep.G_parallel_session_deconfliction: NO OVERLAP with parallel session. Parallel is on S11-D1 track. This option is S10-D2 OOS-side. Race-condition risk is LOW (only the cleanup-note canonical path collision risk; trivial to avoid).
option_1_oos_cost_stress_sweep.summary: Run S0/S2/S3 cost tiers (and optionally S4) against the existing OOS data, using the existing OOS driver, to test whether IS-side cost-robustness survives the OOS regime.

option_2_s11_d1_mnq_single_instrument_fa.A_informational_gain: MEDIUM-HIGH. Different universe means different trade frequency profile and different binding constraints. Would reveal whether single-instrument Donchian is fundamentally different from multi-asset cross-portfolio Donchian.
option_2_s11_d1_mnq_single_instrument_fa.B_reuse_of_sealed_infrastructure: LOW. Different Tier-N spec, different plan-lock, different drivers, different cache. Reuses framework discipline patterns only.
option_2_s11_d1_mnq_single_instrument_fa.C_risk_profile: MEDIUM. Requires Databento fetch for the long-history MNQ.c.0 cache (DR9-vulnerable if data has gaps, as s10-D1 showed). Vendor dependency + key handling required. Full chain P3 - P11 to execute.
option_2_s11_d1_mnq_single_instrument_fa.D_directness_vs_binding_constraint: INDIRECT. Does NOT extend the S10-D2 evidence. Tests a different research hypothesis on a different universe. The lesson from S10-D2 (window length / sample density) may or may not transfer.
option_2_s11_d1_mnq_single_instrument_fa.E_time_to_result: Slow. Multi-week effort across full chain.
option_2_s11_d1_mnq_single_instrument_fa.F_chain_of_custody_integrity: NEW CHAIN. Starts fresh S11-D1 anchors (Tier-N spec, plan-lock, phase-2 plan). No evidentiary linkage to S10-D2 except by reference.
option_2_s11_d1_mnq_single_instrument_fa.G_parallel_session_deconfliction: HIGH OVERLAP RISK. Parallel session is ACTIVELY iterating on S11-D1 Tier-N spec at 74e254fd. Authorizing Option 2 in this session would put two controllers competing on the same chain. STRONG argument to NOT authorize Option 2 in this session and instead let parallel session continue on S11-D1.
option_2_s11_d1_mnq_single_instrument_fa.note: Parallel session is the natural canonical executor for S11-D1. Best disposition: this session does NOT authorize Option 2; parallel session continues as the S11-D1 owner.
option_2_s11_d1_mnq_single_instrument_fa.summary: Pursue a single-instrument MNQ.c.0 long-history Tier-N candidate with the Donchian breakout strategy, testing whether removing the multi-asset universe sidesteps the sample-density problem.

option_3_longer_oos_window.A_informational_gain: MEDIUM. Directly attacks the binding constraint but only adds ~14% additional history (5 months / 36 months). 5 months at 17.7 trades/year = ~7 additional trades = 60 closed trades total. Still below K9 threshold of 100. Would not move the verdict from INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE.
option_3_longer_oos_window.B_reuse_of_sealed_infrastructure: MEDIUM. Reuses strategy code, IS driver source (after constants update), runner_main.CONFIG. Requires new OOS driver constants (window end date), new cache (or extended cache), and a fresh Databento fetch authorization.
option_3_longer_oos_window.C_risk_profile: MEDIUM-HIGH. Vendor dependency: requires Databento fetch with separate key-handling authorization (the same boundaries as S10-D1 Step 02b). Failure modes: network transient errors (already observed twice on S10-D1), DR9 fires on cross-symbol continuity (already killed S10-D1).
option_3_longer_oos_window.D_directness_vs_binding_constraint: MOST DIRECT but INSUFFICIENT IN ISOLATION. Adds only ~14% more history; would not push trade count above K9. Only worth doing if combined with another year of history, which is not yet available.
option_3_longer_oos_window.E_time_to_result: Days. Fetch + cache + new OOS driver constants + P10 OOS re-run.
option_3_longer_oos_window.F_chain_of_custody_integrity: EXTENDS the S10-D2 chain with a P10.OOS_EXTENDED phase, or starts a sibling P10' phase. Anchors preserved but a new OOS driver constant change is required.
option_3_longer_oos_window.G_parallel_session_deconfliction: LOW-MEDIUM. No overlap with S11-D1. Possible overlap if parallel session also pursues longer-OOS in future.
option_3_longer_oos_window.note: 5 months of additional history is insufficient to move verdict from INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE. Better to wait until ~2 more years of OOS history accumulates, OR run Option 1 first to inform whether longer OOS is even the right next move.
option_3_longer_oos_window.summary: Extend the OOS window past 2025-12-31 to incorporate additional Databento history (~5 months of bars currently available), directly attacking the OOS sample-size binding constraint.

option_4_fresh_candidate_different_marke.A_informational_gain: VARIABLE. Could be high if the new universe has fundamentally different sample-density characteristics. Could be wasteful if the new universe has the same binding constraint.
option_4_fresh_candidate_different_marke.B_reuse_of_sealed_infrastructure: LOWEST. Full new Tier-N spec, plan-lock, phase-2 plan, drivers, runner, cache.
option_4_fresh_candidate_different_marke.C_risk_profile: HIGHEST. New code surface, new vendor surface, new candidate-specific risks (e.g., currency-pair correlation structures may break K10; agricultural futures may have weather-regime structural breaks).
option_4_fresh_candidate_different_marke.D_directness_vs_binding_constraint: INDIRECT. Tests a different hypothesis entirely. May or may not address the S10-D2 lesson.
option_4_fresh_candidate_different_marke.F_chain_of_custody_integrity: NEW CHAIN. No linkage to S10-D2 except by reference.
option_4_fresh_candidate_different_marke.note: Worth keeping on the research roadmap but not the next move. Should follow at least Option 1's verdict to inform candidate-design priorities (is cost-stress the next thing to check on new candidates? or sample-density per year?).
option_4_fresh_candidate_different_marke.summary: Author an entirely new Tier-N candidate using a different universe (e.g., currency futures, agricultural futures) or a regime-overlay variant on the existing S10-D2 strategy.

parallel_session_activity_acknowledged.implication: Parallel session is independently pursuing Option 2 (s11_d1_mnq_single_instrument_fallback). Authorizing Option 2 in this session would create a direct race condition on the same Tier-N chain. This is taken into account in the recommendation below; it does NOT eliminate Option 2 as a structural option, but informs which track this session should propose for fresh authorization.
relevant_commits: 9c630886 Seal S11-D1 MNQ.c.0 single-instrument Tier-N specification
c110fd4d Add S11-D1 Tier-N spec rev2 drawdown condition fix
74e254fd Add s11 D1 MNQ.c.0 Tier-N spec DRAFT (mechanic F1 trend-no-pyramid RESOLVED)

primary_rationale: Highest informational-gain-per-unit-cost of the four options
Reuses S10-D2 sealed infrastructure 100% (OOS driver, OOS cache, Tier-N spec)
Zero new vendor surface (no fetch, no Databento, no API key access)
Adds ORTHOGONAL evidence to the K9 sample-size finding: tests whether cost-stress robustness observed on IS also survives the OOS regime
Quickest path to a sealed result (minutes; deterministic driver)
Cleanest parallel-session deconfliction: parallel is on S11-D1, not on S10-D2 OOS-side
recommended_authorization_sequence_no_pr: 1. Authorize Option 1 (S10-D2 P10.5 OOS cost-stress sweep) when ready
2. Observe parallel session's progress on S11-D1 (independent; no this-session authorization needed)
3. After Option 1 verdict: re-evaluate whether to pursue Option 3 (longer OOS) or Option 4 (fresh candidate) based on what Option 1 revealed about cost-sensitivity on the OOS regime
secondary_rationale: Parallel session has been iterating on S11-D1 Tier-N spec across three commits (9c630886, c110fd4d, 74e254fd) and is the natural canonical executor
Authorizing S11-D1 in THIS session would compete with parallel session's work on the same chain
Best operator move: let parallel session continue on S11-D1; this session focuses on extending S10-D2 evidence with Option 1
track_3_rationale: Only ~5 months of additional history currently available beyond 2025-12-31
5 months at 17.7 trades/year ~ 7 additional trades = 60 total, still below K9 threshold of 100
Would not move verdict from INSUFFICIENT_SAMPLE in isolation
Worth revisiting in 12-24 months when more OOS history exists, OR after Option 1 verdict reveals whether longer-OOS is the right next attack vector
track_4_rationale: Largest scope; should follow at least one of Options 1 or 2 to inform candidate-design priorities

seal_method: sha256 over json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":"), ensure_ascii=False) EXCLUDING report_seal_sha256 + seal_method

selection_criteria.A_informational_gain_per_unit_new_scope: How much new evidence does the option produce per unit of new code/scope/cost?
selection_criteria.B_reuse_of_existing_sealed_infrastructur: How much of the S10-D2 sealed chain (driver, cache, runner, Tier-N spec) does the option reuse?
selection_criteria.C_risk_profile: Vendor dependency, new code surface, key-handling risk, race-condition risk vs parallel session.
selection_criteria.D_directness_vs_binding_constraint: Does the option directly attack the binding constraint (OOS window length / sample size), or attack it indirectly via a different binding profile?
selection_criteria.E_time_to_result: Wall-clock time from authorization to sealed verdict.
selection_criteria.F_chain_of_custody_integrity: Does the option extend the existing S10-D2 chain (more evidence under same anchors) or start a new chain (new anchors, new Tier-N spec, new plan-lock)?
selection_criteria.G_parallel_session_deconfliction: Does the option overlap with what parallel session is already running, causing scope-merge / race-condition risk?
